_meta.purpose: ONE shared body-zone vocabulary, TWO uses: (1) Mobility — session coverage + optional tightness 1-5 per zone; (2) PT — pain 0-10 per zone (attached to a session or standalone flare-up). Same ids in both places so tightness and pain can be laid over each other in Reflect without any mapping table.
_meta.sidedSemantics: sided=true zones accept side: 'left' | 'right' | 'both' (side optional — omitting it means unspecified/whole zone, which is always a legal, honest answer). sided=false zones are midline structures where left/right is anatomically meaningless at this granularity.
_meta.tenthZoneChoice: glutes chosen over feet: glute/piriformis is one of the most common distinct tightness AND pain loci (sciatica-adjacent, desk sitting, running) and is NOT covered by any neighbor — 'hips' here means the joint/front/groin complex, 'hamstrings' the posterior thigh, 'lower-back' the lumbar midline. Feet (plantar fascia) lost the coin flip because calves-ankles already borders it and plantar issues usually co-report with calf tightness; feet can be added later without disturbing existing ids.

scales.tightness: 1-5, mobility-use only, self-reported, descriptive ('how tight did this feel'), never scored against a target
scales.pain: 0-10 NRS (numeric rating scale), PT-use only, self-reported; 0 = no pain. Standard NRS anchors, no app interpretation of the number (general-wellness guardrail — see pt-model.md)
